text:
Since children have long been acknowledged in the societies and within the legal and judiciary systems as being different, it follows that law and legal institutions must also adapt and change in order to reflect the changing values of the societies of which they are part. Also at the normative and institutional level, there is an acknowledgement that children need special protection and that, because children are not fully socialised members of society, the repressive aspects of the law such as criminal and misdemeanour justice need special consideration.

Children who get involved in criminal actions can be referred to under the modern terms used in juvenile justice as “child in conflict with the law.” This paper examines the legal status of the child in conflict with the law according to contemporary rules on protection of the child in criminal proceedings. It deals with the normative framework for the child in conflict with the law, and the prospects for the future – it takes into account and compares international norms, criteria and standards, which are in line with the needs and situation in Croatian society. Beyond the issue of the child as the author of criminal action, a particular focus is given to the generally considered vulnerable position of the child as the victim of criminal action.
